title: sasr
text: use sasr, need follow steps sascfg() creates ssh based SAS session. password-less ssh connection supported, e.g. ssh via public keys. host hostname SAS server. sas SAS executable path SAS server. arguments added configuration file directly. tunnel rtunnel required want transfer datasets R SAS. Use integers like tunnel = 9999L R, modify sascfg_personal.py make sure integers. can create configuration SAS connection restricted ssh. can multiple configuration files different file names session created session available stored .sasr_env$.sas_session .sasr_env$.sas_session created, session used default. create variable called .sas_session environment sasr:::.sasr_env SAS_session one argument sascfg, pointing SAS session configuration file. use multiple sessions, need store session your_session <- sas_session_ssh(sascfg) Tunneling must enabled transfer datasets. variable names datasets contain dots otherwise SAS may recognize. index (row names) transferred SAS. returned value named list, LST result LOG log file run_sas argument results=, can either “TEXT” “HTML”. argument decides LST format. Transfer SAS datasets back R use sd2df

title: sasr
code: library(sasr) df2sd(mtcars, "mt") result <- run_sas("   proc freq data = mt;   run; ")  cat(result$LOG)  cat(result$LST)
